purpose: The edge-rule head-function forms (the board's named-unobserved 'f(X) in one or both edge-head positions'; only the node-rule form was cased before session 29): one registered head function drives two edge rules through the default engine — LinkedOne(f(X), Y) grounds only its first head position through the resolver, LinkedBoth(f(X), f(Y)) grounds both — and each derives its target on the existing A->B edge with [1,1] (the non-infer-edges path keeps head pairs filtered to existing edges). The edge trace and the per-target edge frames carry the compared derivations.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_head_function (edge-rule forms, named unobserved until session 29); interpretation.py:2273-2314 at the pin (determine_edge_head_vars); reference fn harness/reference_fns.py first_clause_first_grounding; screened live 2026-07-12 (screen_pin_arms.py edge-head-fn + edge-head-fn-both, trace rows byte-identical cross-engine per form)
